instructions.instruction: Use `AskUserQuestion` if image repository/tag, service port, resource limits, probe endpoints, scaling requirements, workload type, or storage requirements are missing or ambiguous.
instructions.original_snippets: REQUIRED: Use `AskUserQuestion` if any of these are missing or ambiguous... Do NOT assume values for critical settings. Ask first, then proceed.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating a Helm chart and one or more critical settings are not provided in the request

instructions.instruction: Use `bash scripts/generate_chart_structure.sh <chart-name> <output-directory> [options]` to scaffold chart structure.
instructions.original_snippets:
Use the scaffolding script:
bash scripts/generate_chart_structure.sh <chart-name> <output-directory> [options]
instructions.relevant_when: When creating a new Helm chart from scratch

instructions.instruction: Pass only the repository name without tag to `--image` (e.g., `redis` not `redis:7-alpine`).
instructions.original_snippets: --image <repo> - Image repository (default: nginx). Note: Pass only the repository name without tag (e.g., `redis` not `redis:7-alpine`)
instructions.relevant_when: When calling the generate_chart_structure.sh script with an image argument

instructions.instruction: Customize port names for non-HTTP services (e.g., `redis`, `mysql`, `grpc`) — the script uses `http` as the default port name.
instructions.original_snippets: The script uses `http` as the default port name in templates. Customize port names for non-HTTP services (e.g., `redis`, `mysql`, `grpc`)
instructions.relevant_when: When generating charts for non-HTTP services

instructions.instruction: Use `bash scripts/generate_standard_helpers.sh <chart-name> <chart-directory>` or the `assets/_helpers-template.tpl` to generate standard helpers.
instructions.original_snippets:
Use the helpers script or `assets/_helpers-template.tpl`:
bash scripts/generate_standard_helpers.sh <chart-name> <chart-directory>
instructions.relevant_when: When creating a new chart and setting up _helpers.tpl

instructions.instruction: Read reference files `references/resource_templates.md`, `references/helm_template_functions.md`, and `references/crd_patterns.md` at template generation time before generating templates.
instructions.original_snippets: You MUST use the `Read` tool to load these reference files at this stage, even if you read them earlier... Do NOT skip this step. Template quality depends on having current reference patterns loaded.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating any Kubernetes resource templates for a Helm chart

instructions.instruction: Use helpers for names and labels: `{{ include "mychart.fullname" . }}` for name and `{{- include "mychart.labels" . | nindent 4 }}` for labels in all templates.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating any Kubernetes resource YAML template

instructions.instruction: Use conditional sections with `with` for optional values like nodeSelector: `{{- with .Values.nodeSelector }}nodeSelector: {{- toYaml . | nindent 2 }}{{- end }}`.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet templates with optional scheduling fields

instructions.instruction: Include checksum annotation in Deployments/StatefulSets/DaemonSets to trigger restarts on ConfigMap/Secret changes: `checksum/config: {{ include (print $.Template.BasePath "/configmap.yaml") . | sha256sum }}`.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating Deployment, StatefulSet, or DaemonSet templates that use ConfigMaps or Secrets

instructions.instruction: Group related settings logically in values.yaml, document every value with `# --` comments, provide sensible defaults, and include security contexts, resource limits, and probes.
instructions.original_snippets:
Structure guidelines:
- Group related settings logically
- Document every value with `# --` comments
- Provide sensible defaults
- Include security contexts, resource limits, probes
instructions.relevant_when: When creating values.yaml for a Helm chart

instructions.instruction: Do NOT hardcode image tags in `values.yaml`; use an empty tag `""` with `appVersion` as the default, overrideable via `--set image.tag=v1.2.3`.
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER hardcode image tags in `values.yaml`... BAD: image: repository: myapp tag: latest... GOOD: image: repository: myapp tag: "" with `appVersion` as the default, overridden via `--set image.tag=v1.2.3`.
instructions.relevant_when: When defining image settings in values.yaml

instructions.instruction: Always set both `requests` and `limits` for CPU and memory in the container spec; never omit the `resources:` block.
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER omit `resources:` limits and requests on containers... BAD: No `resources:` block in the container spec template. GOOD: Set both `requests` and `limits` for CPU and memory, with documented tuning guidance in `values.yaml`.
instructions.relevant_when: When generating any container spec in Deployment, StatefulSet, DaemonSet, Job, or CronJob templates

instructions.instruction: Use `helm upgrade --install --atomic --timeout 5m` in CI/CD pipelines; never use `helm upgrade --install` without `--atomic`.
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER use `helm upgrade --install` without `--atomic` in CI/CD... BAD: `helm upgrade --install myapp ./chart`... GOOD: `helm upgrade --install --atomic --timeout 5m myapp ./chart`
instructions.relevant_when: When generating CI/CD pipeline scripts or deployment scripts that invoke helm upgrade

instructions.instruction: Use a layered values approach: `values.yaml` for defaults, environment-specific `values-prod.yaml` for overrides; never place environment-specific values inside the chart's default `values.yaml`.
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER place environment-specific values inside the chart's default `values.yaml`... GOOD: Use a layered values approach: `values.yaml` for defaults, `values-prod.yaml` for overrides, `-f values-prod.yaml` at deploy time.
instructions.relevant_when: When designing chart values structure for multi-environment deployments

instructions.instruction: Run `helm template . | kubeval --strict --ignore-missing-schemas` (or kubeconform) after `helm lint` to validate rendered manifests; never rely solely on `helm lint`.
instructions.original_snippets: NEVER skip `helm template` + `kubeval`/`kubeconform` validation... BAD: Only run `helm lint` before deploying. GOOD: `helm template . | kubeval --strict --ignore-missing-schemas` to validate rendered manifests.
instructions.relevant_when: When documenting or scripting the chart validation workflow

instructions.instruction: Ship CRDs in the `crds/` directory (not templated); template CR instances in `templates/`.
instructions.original_snippets: Ship CRDs in `crds/` directory (not templated); template CR instances in `templates/`.
instructions.relevant_when: When creating Helm charts that bundle Custom Resource Definitions

instructions.instruction: When converting raw Kubernetes manifests to Helm: parameterize names using helpers, extract values, apply label/conditional patterns, use `toYaml` for complex objects, and create `_helpers.tpl` with standard helpers.
instructions.original_snippets:
Converting Manifests to Helm:
1. Parameterize names (use helpers) and extract values
2. Apply label/conditional patterns, use `toYaml` for complex objects
3. Create `_helpers.tpl` with standard helpers
4. Validate with devops-skills:helm-validator
instructions.relevant_when: When migrating existing raw Kubernetes YAML manifests into a Helm chart

instructions.instruction: After generating charts, invoke `devops-skills:helm-validator` to ensure quality (helm lint, template render, schema checks, dry-run).
instructions.original_snippets: After generating charts, invoke devops-skills:helm-validator to ensure quality.
instructions.relevant_when: After completing chart generation

instructions.instruction: Use `required` to enforce required values, `default` for fallback values, `quote` for strings, `include` to use helpers, `toYaml` to convert complex objects, `tpl` to render values as templates, and `nindent` for newline + indent.
instructions.original_snippets: Template Functions Quick Reference... required, default, quote, include, toYaml, tpl, nindent
instructions.relevant_when: When writing Helm template expressions

instructions.instruction: Use templates with checksum annotations conditionally enabled via `.Values.configMap.enabled` and `.Values.secret.enabled`.
instructions.original_snippets: Templates include checksum annotations for ConfigMap/Secret changes (conditionally enabled via `.Values.configMap.enabled` and `.Values.secret.enabled`)
instructions.relevant_when: When generating templates that reference ConfigMaps or Secrets
